replies.text: If you want to make it a colour-thing then that's up to you but it shows the mindset of some of you when you'd prefer Bucky to be Captain America when he's still under mk-ultra and hasn't got himself back together yet. Black Americans have every right to be Captain America as do native Americans but it's only the European set that are allowed to make that decision, it seems. Even though they've made an alternate female version nothing was challenged. Truthfully, I'd prefer that Cap stayed or the mantle was left vacant but this move shows how you guys really think about black Americans in particular and black people in general. Unnecessary hatred for ego-boosting. Another thing is that you can't even see that it's their plan to deliberately antagonise the true fans of these comics by giving access to audiences that weren't interested in this genre, the reigns and just run off with it. The black man has been dying in movies for yonks and has been made the villain throughout history, now you see that it ain't fun when everything is reversed. The black community didn't ask for any of these changes as we don't have that authority but you already know that but don't care as it's easier and more healing for yourselves to just attack and insult. Here's the truth if you like it or not: it's only going to get worse plus you'll look to us for support when forgetting how you really think of us.

​​​​​​​@brocKainThe post modernist trend of calling every scrutiny or friction "toxic", or to see "trauma" everywhere in life, is low resolution and lazy.

Bring forth your counter arguments and debate! Disney writers, and many others in Tinsel Town for that matter, love to ignore or take creative liberties with intellectual properties. Sure that's fine because they now own some of those IPs, but decisions have consequences because that's how life works. 

It turns out that generations of people who love comics (clearly more than the Hollywood writers) won't just tolerate the mismanagement of their beloved characters or childhoods - shocker!! It's called accountability. We don't just get to willy-nilly muttle up enterprises that rely on a human fanbase tied to people's childhoods and get away scot free. 

This goes for the MCU, Little Mermaid, Doctor Who, Snow-white  and any IP that transcends generational impact. People will vote with the almighty dollar and the auto-corrective forces of nature will bring about a well deserved bankruptcy to these companies. Consequences - consequences - consequences. Get over it.

replies.author: @Otto81
replies.text: ​@Mainlyeverything Cap accepted his destiny at the end of Age of Ultron. THAT was a good character arc.  In First Avenger, he proved himself willing to sacrifice himself, much like Tony did time and time again. Then in Endgame he's more than happy to let other people do it so he can do what no one else can or has done in these movies: Go back in time to live a happier life.   "You're not the guy to make the sacrifice play, to lay down on a wire and let the other guy crawl over you" he says in Avengers.... meanwhile, Tony WAS the man willing to make the sacrifice, and Steve just wanted to go back and live with his girlfrield while everyone else either dies or is forced to live on putting themselves at risk as heroes. He's Captain America: The WORST Avenger.

replies.author: @Otto81
replies.text: @ So only Cap, after lecturing Tony on how the REAL heroes are willing to lay their lives on the line, gets a happy ending, while everyone else must carry on putting themselves at risk as heroes... IF they survived in the first place. Steve went from the true leader of the MCU to nothing better than Bruce in the Dark Knight Trilogy: Pine after an ex girlfriend, and run away at the end and wash his hands of all responsibility.

replies.author: @nomercy4186
replies.text: @Otto81  just because he didn’t die doesn’t mean he didn’t go through as much if not more than any other person on the team. Not to mention he was constantly the one being the leader assembling the entire team with nick fury. He had his future stripped away from him with Peggy being trapped in the ice…. Not to mention in terms of logistics, iron man was the only one who COULD get the stones and snap his fingers with his nano tech so you also have that conundrum

replies.author: @Otto81
replies.text: @nomercy4186 you make a good point but I would still argue that Cap set the example of self sacrifice and leadership. Escaping back through time negates that aspect of his character. All of us have dealt with loss and the burden of responsibility in our lives but we don't get to just travel back in time to get do overs. Maybe I'm too harsh, but this was the same problem I had with the ending of Dark Knight Rises. I don't know who that was, but it certainly wasn't freaking Batman.
